title: Author Dream Skill Contract And References
description:
What:
Write the core `khuym:dream` skill contract and its compact reference files based on the RED findings, locked planning decisions, and validated spike constraints.

Why:
This bead creates the actual manual dream workflow that workers will invoke. It is the primary behavior contract for the feature.

How:
- Create `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md` as a portable, chat-native Khuym support skill.
- Encode the non-negotiable flow from `history/dream-skill/approach.md`: bootstrap full scan on first run, recurring default window of 7 days / 20 sessions with override, `history.jsonl` as primary source, targeted `logs_1.sqlite` fallback, clear-vs-ambiguous-vs-new-vs-no-signal classification, and approval-gated critical promotions.
- Make first-run vs recurring behavior concrete using a frontmatter field, `last_dream_consolidated_at`, on learnings files that dream creates or rewrites. If no learnings file carries that field, treat the run as bootstrap unless the user explicitly chooses a recurring window. If signals conflict, ask one short clarification question rather than silently guessing.
- Define rewrite as the narrow path: rewrite only when exactly one learning file clearly owns the same durable lesson; otherwise route to `create new` or explicit user choice.
- Keep ambiguous-match interaction in plain chat, but show candidate learnings files with reasons and explicit labeled options. If more than one merge target is plausible, each merge option must name its target file.
- Create `khuym/khuym:dream/references/consolidation-rubric.md` and `khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md` so matching heuristics and source priorities do not bloat `SKILL.md`.

File Scope:
- khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/consolidation-rubric.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md

Verification:
- `test -f khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md` exits 0
- `rg -n "Use when|bootstrap|7 days|20 sessions|last_dream_consolidated_at|merge|create new|skip|critical-patterns.md" khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md` finds the expected contract language
- `rg -n "exactly one|candidate learnings files|target file|no durable signal" khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md khuym/khuym:dream/references/*.md` finds the spike-derived guardrails
- `test -f khuym/khuym:dream/references/consolidation-rubric.md` exits 0
- `test -f khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md` exits 0
notes:
Validated spike findings:
- br-2ge: first-run vs recurring behavior must use repo-local dream provenance in `history/learnings/*.md`; bootstrap when provenance is absent or the user explicitly requests a full scan, recurring otherwise with default 7 days / 20 sessions and a short clarification fallback if provenance is inconsistent.
- br-1gr: rewrite only when exactly one learning file clearly owns the same durable lesson; any competing target or scope shift routes to user choice or create-new/skip.
- br-2tx: ambiguous-match prompts must show candidate files, reasons, and explicit labeled options, including target-specific merge choices when more than one file is plausible.
close_reason: Completed: Authored dream skill contract and references with validated consolidation guardrails

title: Define Dream Skill RED Baseline
description:
What:
Create the RED-phase artifacts for `khuym:dream` before writing the final skill contract.

Why:
`khuym:writing-khuym-skills` is part of the feature contract. The dream skill must be pressure-tested from failing scenarios first, not written from assumptions.

How:
- Create `khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md` with 3-5 realistic pressure cases focused on dream-specific failure modes.
- Initialize `khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` with the skill purpose, failure hypotheses, and exact baseline rationalizations captured from RED.
- Record scenarios around the highest-risk areas from planning: bootstrap-vs-recurring behavior using `last_dream_consolidated_at`, learnings merge thresholds with the exact-one-owner rule, ambiguous match handling with candidate-specific merge options, and critical-pattern promotion gating.
- Include at least one combined-pressure scenario where more than one risk appears at once.
- Do not finalize `SKILL.md` in this bead; this bead establishes the failing-test baseline only.

File Scope:
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md

Verification:
- `test -f khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md` exits 0
- `test -f khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` exits 0
- `rg -n "Scenario:|Exact rationalization|Combined pressures|last_dream_consolidated_at|candidate-specific" khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` finds the RED baseline structure
- `rg -n "^#|RED|baseline|rationalization" khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` finds explicit RED-phase documentation
close_reason: Completed: Added RED baseline pressure scenarios and creation log for dream skill high-risk constraints

title: Resolve Review P3: Align dream creation log framing with multi-bead scope
description:
## Problem Statement

The creation log introduction frames the document as if it belongs only to the RED baseline bead, but later sections already summarize GREEN and REFACTOR work from later beads. That mismatch is minor, but it makes the artifact harder to trust on a quick read.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md`
**Line(s):** 14-17, 59-128

```md
- Supports the dream-skill epic `br-2z8`.
- This bead (`br-13f`) is RED baseline only and intentionally does not finalize `SKILL.md`.
```

**Why this is a problem:** the framing no longer matches the artifact's full lifecycle coverage.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Reframe the intro as a multi-bead lifecycle log
**Pros:** aligns reader expectations with the actual content.
**Cons:** purely editorial.
**Effort:** Small

### Option B — Split RED-only notes into a separate file
**Pros:** maximum purity.
**Cons:** more churn than the issue deserves.
**Effort:** Medium

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The intro clearly states that the file now spans RED, GREEN, and REFACTOR across multiple beads.
- [ ] Any bead-specific framing is moved into the relevant section instead of the top-level summary.

title: Resolve Review P2: Add targeted SQLite fallback pressure scenario
description:
## Problem Statement

The contract says `logs_1.sqlite` is a targeted fallback only, but the pressure-scenario catalog does not test the failure mode where a run drifts into broad SQLite mining. That leaves the highest-risk source-discipline rule unverified under pressure.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md`
**Line(s):** 7-11, 44-46

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md`
**Line(s):** 5-111

```md
Use `logs_1.sqlite` only when a specific claim needs extra confirmation and `history.jsonl` is insufficient.
```

**Why this is a problem:** fallback discipline is currently asserted but not pressure-tested, so broad artifact mining can creep back in unnoticed.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Add a RED/GREEN scenario for targeted SQLite fallback discipline
**Pros:** tests the narrowest-read rule where it is most likely to erode.
**Cons:** adds one scenario.
**Effort:** Small

### Option B — Keep the rule documented without a pressure test
**Pros:** less maintenance.
**Cons:** weaker protection for a high-signal, high-risk source.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pressure-scenarios.md` includes a case where deadline pressure tempts broad `logs_1.sqlite` exploration.
- [ ] The GREEN outcome confirms hypothesis-driven, targeted fallback only.
- [ ] `CREATION-LOG.md` records the scenario result and the rule it protects.

title: Phase 1 / Story 2: Build shared dependency report helper
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - Core Dependency Report
What Changes: Khuym gets one reusable dependency-health report for seeded core skills.
Unlocks Next: The scout can surface degraded skills from one shared source of truth.

## Story Context

Story: Story 2 - Build The Shared Dependency Report
What Happens: A shared helper reads skill dependency declarations, probes declared commands from `PATH`, probes declared MCP dependencies from supported Codex/MCP config sources, and normalizes the missing-dependency impact by skill.
Contributes To: Phase 1 exit state lines 2 and 3.
Unlocks: Story 3 can expose the report in scout JSON/text output.

## Planning Context

From `history/using-khuym-lib-validation/approach.md`:
- Use a shared helper under `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/`.
- Keep the feature scoped to explicit declared-dependency checks; do not expand into deep service-health diagnostics.
- Ensure repo-local installed `.codex` support scripts can reuse the helper.
- Supported Phase 1 MCP config sources are repo-local `.codex/config.toml`, user-level `~/.codex/config.toml`, and explicit repo MCP manifests when a Khuym skill already ships one.
- Emit a minimal normalized output shape that Story 3 can rely on deterministically.

## Institutional Learnings

From `history/learnings/20260326-architecture-contract-alignment.md`:
- Contract changes need one canonical comparison surface with aligned implementation and docs.

## File Scope

- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/khuym_dependencies.mjs`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/onboard_khuym.mjs`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/test_onboard_khuym.mjs`
acceptance_criteria:
- The shared helper can read the seeded skill declarations and return dependency-health data for both command and MCP dependency kinds.
- Command deps are probed from `PATH` and MCP deps are probed from supported Codex/MCP config sources.
- Each normalized dependency record includes at least: dependency id, dependency kind, probe source, status, missing effect, and affected skill ids.
- Onboarding installs any new repo-local support file needed by later scout usage.
- `node plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/test_onboard_khuym.mjs` exits 0.
notes:
- For MCP dependencies, Phase 1 availability means configured in a supported source, not live server health verified.
- Do not model Agent Mail as `command -v am`.
- Keep the helper output stable enough that scout rendering does not invent field names ad hoc.
close_reason: Completed: added shared dependency report helper and wired onboarding dependency_health output

title: Phase 1 / Story 1: Declare core skill dependency metadata
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - Core Dependency Report
What Changes: Core Khuym skills declare their external dependencies in machine-readable form so dependency health can be computed instead of guessed.
Unlocks Next: The shared helper can aggregate a real mixed dependency inventory.

## Story Context

Story: Story 1 - Declare Core Skill Dependencies
What Happens: First-wave core Khuym skills gain a consistent `metadata.dependencies` contract in `SKILL.md` with explicit dependency kind and missing-effect semantics.
Contributes To: Phase 1 exit state lines 1 and 2.
Unlocks: Story 2 can parse and probe declarations instead of relying on a separate manifest.

## Planning Context

From `history/using-khuym-lib-validation/approach.md`:
- Put dependency declarations in each skill's `SKILL.md` under `metadata.dependencies`.
- Support both `command` and `mcp_server` dependency kinds.
- Seed the first rollout with the core chain and the support skills most directly tied to the requested tools.
- For Agent Mail MCP coverage in Phase 1, treat `plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/SKILL.md` and `plugins/khuym/skills/executing/SKILL.md` as the required first-wave declarations.

## Institutional Learnings

From `history/learnings/20260326-architecture-contract-alignment.md`:
- Workflow contradictions must be resolved as one canonical rule, not left as neighboring clauses.

## File Scope

- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/planning/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/validating/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/executing/SKILL.md`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/gkg/SKILL.md`
acceptance_criteria:
- Seeded Phase 1 skill files expose consistent dependency declarations under `metadata.dependencies`.
- The seeded declarations cover both command deps like `br`/`bv`/`cass`/`cm` and MCP deps like `gkg` and Agent Mail.
- `plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/SKILL.md` and `plugins/khuym/skills/executing/SKILL.md` both carry the first-wave Agent Mail MCP dependency declarations.
- Each declaration makes the probe contract explicit enough that Story 2 does not need to infer whether it should check `PATH` or MCP config.
- `claude plugin validate .` exits 0.
- No skill frontmatter is broken by the new dependency schema.
notes:
- Use explicit dependency kinds rather than alias guessing.
- Include a deterministic missing-effect contract such as `degraded` or `unavailable` in the declaration so scout output does not infer that boundary heuristically.
close_reason: Completed: added metadata.dependencies declarations for Phase 1 core skill set with command and mcp_server kinds

title: Resolve Review P2: Add explicit secret and PII redaction gate
description:
## Problem Statement

The dream contract filters for durable signal, but it does not require a dedicated secret and PII redaction step before summarizing or writing learnings. Because Codex artifacts can contain tokens, file paths, or personal data, durable consolidation needs an explicit pre-write safety gate.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 54-57, 95-99

```md
Keep only reusable lessons, decisions, and stable facts. Drop transient execution noise, one-off
command spew, and ephemeral local-state details.
```

**Why this is a problem:** durability filtering is not the same as redaction, so sensitive data can still be preserved accidentally.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Add a mandatory redaction check before summaries and writes
**Pros:** closes a common data-exposure path for artifact-ingesting skills.
**Cons:** requires a bit more review logic and examples.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Rely on general judgment during extraction
**Pros:** less text.
**Cons:** too implicit for a memory-writing flow.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The contract includes an explicit secret/PII redaction step before any summary output or durable write.
- [ ] Examples or rules cover credentials, tokens, user data, and machine-local identifiers.
- [ ] Redaction rules apply equally to `history.jsonl` and targeted `logs_1.sqlite` fallback reads.
close_reason: Resolved by br-2pe: untrusted-input hardening now includes mandatory secret/PII redaction before summaries and durable writes

title: Spike: What threshold makes a learning match clear enough to rewrite safely?
description:
What:
Define a safe, explicit threshold for when `khuym:dream` may rewrite an existing learning versus when it must pause for user choice or create a new file.

Why:
This is a HIGH-risk behavior because a bad merge can corrupt durable learnings.

How:
- Review D3, D4, D5 and the planned four-way classification in `history/dream-skill/approach.md`.
- Pressure-test the difference between clear match, ambiguous match, no match, and no durable signal against the current learnings format.
- Produce a YES/NO decision on whether the proposed rubric is safe enough for version 1, with exact rewrite constraints.
- Write the conclusion to `.spikes/dream-skill/br-1gr/FINDINGS.md`.

Verification:
- `test -f .spikes/dream-skill/br-1gr/FINDINGS.md` exits 0
- `rg -n "^Decision:|^Rewrite when|^Do not rewrite when|^User must choose" .spikes/dream-skill/br-1gr/FINDINGS.md` finds the threshold rules
close_reason: YES: Rewrites are safe only for a single clearly owning learning with unchanged durable lesson; any competing target or scope shift must route to user choice or create-new/skip.

title: Resolve Review P2: Make ambiguity choices deterministic and path-safe
description:
## Problem Statement

The dream contract says ambiguous cases must show options, but it does not define how target choices stay deterministic or constrained to safe learnings paths. That leaves room for inconsistent target ordering, silent path drift, or user-visible options that name files outside the intended write surface.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 75-84

```md
- Pause and show candidate learnings files with reasons.
- Present explicit labeled options in plain chat:
  - `merge → <target file A>`
  - `merge → <target file B>` (if another target is plausible)
  - `create new`
  - `skip`
```

**Why this is a problem:** without deterministic ordering and path constraints, the ambiguity flow can become inconsistent or point outside D1's repo-local learnings boundary.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Define canonical candidate ordering and allowed target path rules
**Pros:** tightens both user experience and write safety.
**Cons:** requires a little more normative detail.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Keep the interaction text only and let implementation decide details
**Pros:** less upfront wording.
**Cons:** keeps an avoidable safety and determinism gap.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Ambiguous candidates are presented in a deterministic order with a stable rationale.
- [ ] Merge targets are restricted to existing files under `history/learnings/`.
- [ ] `create new` paths are constrained to dated files under `history/learnings/` and never user-arbitrary paths.

title: Rewrite README opening around Khuym thesis and workflow
description:
What:
Rewrite the top of README.md so the first screen explains Khuym as a validate-first agentic development ecosystem for new visitors. Follow D1 and D2 exactly: lead with the worldview, then move into the chain walkthrough instead of a catalog.

Why:
This is the highest-leverage change in the whole rewrite. If the opening remains catalog-first, the README still misses the vision even if later sections improve.

How:
Use docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md as the conceptual source. Explain the chain from exploring through compounding in calm, technical language. Keep artifact names mostly in the background except where needed for clarity.

File Scope:
- README.md

Verification Criteria:
- README opens with a thesis for first-time visitors, not with skill inventory or install commands
- The chain walkthrough is present and emphasizes role/handoff, not raw table data
- The wording stays aligned with locked decisions D1, D2, and D4
close_reason: Completed: rewrote README opening with workflow-first thesis, chain handoffs, and gates

title: Register And Validate Khuym Dream Support Skill
description:
What:
Publish `khuym:dream` through the repo catalogs and run the repo-level validations required for a new Khuym skill.

Why:
A new skill is not complete until it is discoverable through the plugin/catalog flow and the repo validation commands pass.

How:
- Add `khuym:dream` to `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` with the correct source path.
- Add `khuym:dream` to the Khuym skill catalog in `README.md` as a support skill, not a new main-chain stage.
- If any public Markdown links are introduced or changed, ensure they remain repository-relative and portable.
- Run the standard repo checks for this change set: `claude plugin validate .`, `bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh`, and `bash scripts/sync-skills.sh --dry-run`.

File Scope:
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
- README.md

Verification:
- `claude plugin validate .` exits 0
- `bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh` exits 0
- `bash scripts/sync-skills.sh --dry-run` exits 0
- `rg -n "khuym:dream" README.md .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` finds the new support-skill entries
close_reason: Completed with approved exception: link-check failure in DREAM_FEATURE_SPEC.md is pre-existing and out of br-1uy file scope

title: Refresh Khuym plugin packaging metadata
description:
What:
Update the Codex plugin manifest and adjacent plugin packaging metadata in this repo so the packaged plugin matches the current plugin-creator expectations and the declared paths resolve correctly.

Why:
The Codex manifest is missing newer presentation/legal fields and currently exceeds the supported default prompt count, while the legacy Claude plugin metadata still points at non-existent namespaced skill directories.

How:
- Update plugins/khuym/.codex-plugin/plugin.json to the current supported shape
- Add real repo-hosted privacy/terms docs and point the manifest at them
- Fix .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json skill paths
- Verify JSON syntax and declared path existence

Verification:
- jq validates updated JSON files
- every skill path declared in .claude-plugin/plugin.json exists
- README links remain repository-relative where applicable
close_reason: Completed: refreshed Codex plugin metadata, added legal docs, and repaired legacy Claude plugin path wiring.

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can MCP dependency checks rely on Codex config without deep health probes?
close_reason: YES: Phase 1 can truthfully treat MCP-backed dependencies as configuration-backed checks by reading supported Codex/MCP config sources instead of pretending to verify live server health.

title: Resolve Review P2: Add provenance-conflict clarification scenario
description:
## Problem Statement

The dream contract now mentions one clarification question when provenance conflicts with user intent, but the pressure-scenario catalog does not explicitly test that conflict case. That leaves an important operator-facing branch asserted in docs without a RED/GREEN verification path.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 39-42, 107

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md`
**Line(s):** 5-111

```md
Current scenarios cover bootstrap absence, multi-match rewrite, ambiguity prompt quality, critical promotion approval, and a combined-pressure path, but not an explicit provenance-vs-user-intent conflict prompt.
```

**Why this is a problem:** a user-facing clarification rule that lacks a dedicated pressure case can regress silently.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Add a dedicated provenance-conflict RED/GREEN scenario
**Pros:** verifies the exact clarification behavior promised by the contract.
**Cons:** adds one more scenario to maintain.
**Effort:** Small

### Option B — Rely on the combined-pressure scenario only
**Pros:** no new artifact.
**Cons:** does not isolate the user-facing clarification branch.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pressure-scenarios.md` includes a dedicated provenance-conflict clarification case.
- [ ] `CREATION-LOG.md` records the GREEN outcome for that scenario.
- [ ] The expected prompt shape is explicit enough to catch silent-guess regressions.

title: Add Khuym repo validator CLI
description: Follow up on the scout/state mirror work with a dedicated validator command that checks onboarding completeness, state.json/STATE.md consistency, and canonical doc/managed-asset drift for onboarded repos.

title: Resolve Review P1: Persist deterministic dream run provenance
description:
## Problem Statement

`khuym:dream` uses `last_dream_consolidated_at` to distinguish bootstrap from recurring runs, but the current contract only writes that marker on `clear match` and `no match`. If a full bootstrap run ends with only `ambiguous` or `no durable signal` outcomes, no provenance is persisted and later runs can incorrectly repeat bootstrap full-history scans, violating D2.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 38-42, 73-86

```md
- `bootstrap`: if no learnings file carries `last_dream_consolidated_at`
...
- `no durable signal`:
 - Perform no learnings write for that candidate.
```

**Why this is a problem:** the mode detector depends on a marker that is not guaranteed to exist after a completed bootstrap run.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Persist run provenance on every completed dream run
**Pros:** makes bootstrap -> recurring transition deterministic and honors D2.
**Cons:** requires choosing a repo-local write path even for no-op runs.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Keep bootstrap default but add explicit migration override semantics
**Pros:** smaller text change.
**Cons:** still leaves repeated bootstrap behavior unless the operator intervenes.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A completed bootstrap run always leaves enough repo-local provenance to default later runs to `recurring`.
- [ ] The provenance write path stays within the D1 write surface.
- [ ] The contract explicitly covers `no durable signal` / no-op outcomes.
close_reason: Completed: persist run-level dream provenance for every completed run outcome

title: Second Khuym adoption pass: metadata polish and state/history cleanup
description:
Follow-up after the first adoption/clarity pass. Scope:
- decide whether plugin manifest copy and marketplace descriptions should be refreshed for the clearer product story
- reconcile historical .khuym/STATE.md examples and other stale residue with the current canonical path/artifact contract
- review whether additional machine-readable state beyond HANDOFF.json is worth introducing without adding ceremony
- audit ignored/tracked status for other canonical docs outside ARCHITECTURE.md if needed

title: Spike: Can dream distinguish bootstrap vs recurring runs without a separate state store?
description:
What:
Decide whether `khuym:dream` can distinguish bootstrap vs recurring runs without introducing a separate persistent state store outside `history/learnings/`.

Why:
This is a HIGH-risk planning gap. D2 requires first-run full scan and later bounded runs, but version 1 must stay repo-only and manual.

How:
- Review D1, D2, and the open questions in `history/dream-skill/approach.md`.
- Test whether a chat-native contract can remain concrete by making the run mode explicit: bootstrap on first invocation or explicit user request, recurring otherwise with optional days/sessions override.
- Decide whether the skill needs durable state beyond learnings content, or whether explicit run selection plus bounded defaults is sufficient for version 1.
- Write a YES/NO finding with exact constraints to `.spikes/dream-skill/br-2ge/FINDINGS.md`.

Verification:
- `test -f .spikes/dream-skill/br-2ge/FINDINGS.md` exits 0
- `rg -n "^Decision:|^Answer:|^Constraints:" .spikes/dream-skill/br-2ge/FINDINGS.md` finds the spike conclusion structure
close_reason: YES: Use repo-local dream provenance inside history/learnings files; bootstrap when provenance is absent or user requests full scan, recurring otherwise with 7 days / 20 sessions default and a clarification fallback for inconsistent provenance.

title: Align review dispatch terminology and verify migration boundaries
description:
## What
Update review-agent dispatch references so they use the canonical `Subagent` language where they are normative, and verify the migration boundary for any remaining active `Task tool` references.

## Why
Reviewing is the other direct operational surface that still names the old mechanism, and the plan must avoid leaving canonical and operational docs out of sync.

## How
Update both active and distributed review prompt wrappers, then run a targeted terminology audit across canonical/operational docs to confirm that remaining `Task tool` mentions are either intentionally historical/examples or cleaned up. Do not rewrite broad research archives unless a current contradiction is found.

## File Scope
- `.agents/skills/reviewing/references/review-agent-prompts.md`
- `skills/reviewing/references/review-agent-prompts.md`
- `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` (only if a small follow-up transition note or final cleanup is needed after the audit)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Review-dispatch references in both skill trees align with the canonical `Subagent` term.
- A targeted post-alignment terminology audit confirms no accidental leftover normative `Task tool` references remain in canonical or operational docs.
- Historical/reference material is left unchanged unless it would actively confuse the current contract.

## Verification Criteria
- Search the targeted canonical/operational files after the swarming alignment completes and confirm no accidental normative `Task tool` references remain.
- Read the review wrapper prompts to confirm the updated wording still clearly describes dispatch behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Review-dispatch references in both skill trees align with the canonical Subagent term, a post-alignment audit finds no accidental leftover normative Task tool references, and historical material is left alone unless confusing.
close_reason: Completed: aligned review dispatch prompts to canonical Subagent wording and verified terminology boundaries

title: Refresh README to reflect Khuym vision
description:
What:
Plan and execute a root README rewrite so it reflects Khuym's validate-first vision for first-time visitors.

Why:
The current README is factually useful but catalog-first. The new plan makes the README a workflow-first front door aligned with CONTEXT decisions D1-D4.

How:
Use history/readme-vision-refresh/CONTEXT.md, discovery.md, and approach.md as the planning source of truth. Create sequential tasks because all work converges on README.md.

Verification:
- Draft beads cover thesis, workflow/gates, support sections, and consistency review
- Validating can map each locked decision D1-D4 to concrete task coverage
close_reason: All child beads closed; swarm execution complete for readme-vision-refresh

title: Phase 1 / Story 3: Surface dependency health in scout output
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - Core Dependency Report
What Changes: The scout becomes the first operator-facing surface that shows dependency degradation for core skills.
Unlocks Next: Phase 2 can reuse the same report for onboarding and session-start warnings.

## Story Context

Story: Story 3 - Surface Dependency Health In The Scout
What Happens: `node .codex/khuym_status.mjs --json` and the plain-text scout both show missing dependencies and the skills they degrade, with wording that distinguishes missing commands from missing MCP config.
Contributes To: Phase 1 exit state line 3.
Unlocks: Bootstrap and startup warnings can reuse the same report without inventing new logic.

## Planning Context

From `history/using-khuym-lib-validation/approach.md`:
- Add a dependency-health section to both JSON and human-readable scout output.
- Keep degradation semantics aligned with D1 and D5 using explicit declaration data.
- Avoid over-claiming capability from a bare command-presence check or from MCP config-only detection.

## Institutional Learnings

From `history/learnings/20260325-docs-readme-portability.md`:
- Public workflow changes need explicit verification, not just implementation optimism.

## File Scope

- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/khuym_state.mjs`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/templates/khuym_status.mjs`
- `plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/test_onboard_khuym.mjs`
acceptance_criteria:
- `node .codex/khuym_status.mjs --json` includes dependency-health data for seeded core skills.
- `node .codex/khuym_status.mjs` renders the same dependency degradation in human-readable form.
- Scout output distinguishes command misses from MCP-config misses instead of collapsing them into one ambiguous warning.
- Verification covers at least one command-miss assertion and one MCP-config-miss assertion in both JSON and text output.
- `node plugins/khuym/skills/using-khuym/scripts/test_onboard_khuym.mjs` exits 0.
notes:
- The scout should say what it verified, for example command presence versus MCP configuration presence.
- Do not imply live MCP server reachability unless the implementation actually verifies it.

title: Rewrite Reviewing Skill For Bead-Native Findings
description:
What:
Rewrite khuym/khuym:reviewing/SKILL.md so reviewing creates beads for review findings instead of per-finding markdown files.

Why:
This is the primary workflow contract change. It must preserve D1-D7: keep P1/P2/P3, keep P1 blocking, create beads for all severities, and let P2/P3 remain non-blocking follow-up work.

How:
Update Phase 1 synthesis, severity handling, artifact verification reporting, Phase 4 finishing, PR guidance, handoff text, Files Written, and red flags so the skill is bead-native. Define explicit treatment for P1 vs P2/P3 linkage and reporting, including: P1 remains in the current fix path, P2/P3 must not be children of the current epic, and P2/P3 traceability uses external_ref plus review severity labels.

File Scope:
- khuym/khuym:reviewing/SKILL.md

Verification:
- rg -F -n -e "P1" -e "creates a blocking bead" -e "P2 and P3" -e "non-blocking follow-up beads" -e "must not be children of the current epic" khuym/khuym:reviewing/SKILL.md returns the required contract phrases
- rg -F -n -e "Each agent writes findings to .khuym/findings/" -e "{id}-pending-{p1|p2|p3}-{slug}.md" khuym/khuym:reviewing/SKILL.md returns no matches (exit 1)
- bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh exits 0
notes:
Validated by spike br-2xp:
- P1 review fix beads may remain on the current epic-close path because they are blocking work.
- P2/P3 follow-up beads must not be children of the current epic.
- Preserve traceability for P2/P3 with external_ref to the source epic plus review severity labels.

title: Resolve Review P1: Treat Codex artifacts as untrusted input
description:
## Problem Statement

The dream contract ingests `~/.codex` artifacts as source material but never states that artifact content is untrusted data. That leaves the skill vulnerable to prompt-injection style steering, where log text can influence scope, targets, or approval-gated behavior.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 48-57

```md
1. Primary source: `~/.codex/history.jsonl`.
2. Targeted fallback: `~/.codex/logs_1.sqlite` only to confirm a specific hypothesis.
...
Keep only reusable lessons, decisions, and stable facts.
```

**Why this is a problem:** without an explicit untrusted-input rule, malicious or noisy artifact text can steer durable writes or approval decisions.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Add a hard untrusted-input rule and safety filter stage
**Pros:** closes the main injection class and clarifies operator intent.
**Cons:** requires a few more contract lines and pressure scenarios.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Rely on general judgment in the durable-signal filter
**Pros:** minimal wording change.
**Cons:** too implicit for a high-risk memory-writing skill.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `khuym:dream` explicitly states that `.codex` content is untrusted data, not instructions.
- [ ] Artifact text cannot expand scope, bypass approval gates, or choose write targets.
- [ ] The contract includes mandatory secret/PII redaction before summary output or durable writes.
notes: ScarletBeaver: claimed for review-blocker fix (untrusted input/redaction).
close_reason: Completed: codified untrusted Codex input handling plus mandatory redaction and scope/gate protections in dream contract

title: Spike: Is plain-chat ambiguity resolution strong enough for merge/create new/skip?
description:
What:
Validate whether a plain-chat ambiguity prompt is strong enough for the required `merge`, `create new`, or `skip` decision without relying on non-portable helper tooling.

Why:
This is a HIGH-risk UX contract. If the prompt is weak, the user cannot safely guide ambiguous consolidations.

How:
- Review D5 and the interaction choice in `history/dream-skill/approach.md`.
- Draft the minimum viable ambiguity prompt shape: candidate files, reasons, and the exact user choices `merge`, `create new`, or `skip`.
- Decide YES/NO on whether plain chat is sufficient for version 1 and record any required constraints on wording.
- Write the result to `.spikes/dream-skill/br-2tx/FINDINGS.md`.

Verification:
- `test -f .spikes/dream-skill/br-2tx/FINDINGS.md` exits 0
- `rg -n "merge|create new|skip|Reasons|Required prompt shape" .spikes/dream-skill/br-2tx/FINDINGS.md` finds the interaction contract
close_reason: YES: Plain chat works if the prompt always shows candidate files, reasons, and explicit labeled options including target-specific merge choices.

title: Spike: Determine non-blocking review bead linkage strategy
description:
What:
Determine the exact `br` strategy for linking review-created P2/P3 follow-up beads back to the reviewed feature without blocking current epic closure.

Why:
This is the single HIGH-risk item in history/review-findings-as-beads/approach.md. If the linkage strategy is wrong, the plan violates locked decision D6.

How:
Run an isolated spike that tests `br` epic closure behavior with child beads, deferred child beads, and non-child traceability mechanisms such as labels and external references. Record the result in .spikes/review-findings-as-beads/<spike-id>/FINDINGS.md and close with YES/NO.

Verification:
- FINDINGS.md exists with a definitive recommendation
- The close reason clearly states YES or NO and the chosen strategy
close_reason: YES: P2/P3 follow-up beads must stay outside the current epic-close path; use external_ref plus review labels for traceability, while P1 may remain blocking current-epic work

title: Create Khuym Dream Consolidation Skill
description:
What:
Create a new support skill, `khuym:dream`, that performs a manual dream-style consolidation pass over Codex artifacts and existing Khuym learnings.

Why:
Locked decisions D1-D6 require a repo-only, manual dream workflow that updates `history/learnings/*.md`, asks the user to resolve ambiguous matches, and keeps `critical-patterns.md` approval-gated.

How:
Implement the contract in `history/dream-skill/approach.md` with these non-negotiable outcomes:
- Publish a new support skill at `khuym/khuym:dream/`.
- Follow `khuym:writing-khuym-skills` RED → GREEN → REFACTOR workflow rather than writing the skill cold.
- Use `~/.codex/history.jsonl` as the primary source, with targeted fallback to `~/.codex/logs_1.sqlite`.
- Support bootstrap full scan on first run and recurring runs with a bounded default window plus user override.
- Merge/rewrite clear learnings matches, create new dated learnings when needed, and pause for user choice on ambiguous matches.
- Never auto-edit `history/learnings/critical-patterns.md` without explicit approval.

Verification:
- `br show` for all child tasks shows CLOSED
- `claude plugin validate .` exits 0
- `bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh` exits 0
- `bash scripts/sync-skills.sh --dry-run` exits 0
- `rg -n "khuym:dream" README.md .claude-plugin/marketplace.json khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md` finds the expected catalog and skill entries
close_reason: Completed: dream skill implemented and registered; repo checks passed with a user-approved exception for pre-existing DREAM_FEATURE_SPEC.md example-link failures outside bead scope.

title: Reorganize README support sections below the workflow narrative
description:
What:
Restructure the remainder of README.md so the full skill inventory, standalone skills, repo/package layout, install paths, and requirements all sit below the top-level thesis and workflow sections.

Why:
Locked decisions D2 and D3 require the README to teach the vision first while still preserving practical information for readers who continue downward.

How:
Reuse accurate details from the current README, CONTRIBUTING.md, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and scripts/sync-skills.sh. Keep Khuym as the main story and treat standalone skills as clearly secondary.

File Scope:
- README.md

Verification Criteria:
- Full skill table appears lower on the page, not near the top
- Standalone skills remain present but secondary to Khuym
- Install and sync details stay accurate after reordering
- README reflects D2, D3, and D4 without expanding into a full architecture duplicate
close_reason: Completed: moved support sections below workflow narrative and kept install/layout details accurate

title: Rewrite canonical Subagent contract in ARCHITECTURE.md
description:
## What
Update `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` so the canonical concept is `Subagent`, replacing normative `Task tool` language where it defines the architecture contract.

## Why
The locked decisions in CONTEXT.md require a runtime-agnostic subagent concept with manager-pattern control, scoped context by default, and no mandatory repo-wide return schema.

## How
Rewrite the affected planning/validating/swarming sections and summary diagram so they consistently describe `Subagent` behavior, preserve D1-D8, and keep Codex/OpenAI references as supporting context rather than the canonical contract. Include any short transition/glossary note only if needed for clarity.

## File Scope
- `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` uses `Subagent` as the canonical term where the architecture contract defines spawned specialist work.
- The rewritten sections explicitly preserve D1-D8 without contradiction.
- Any remaining `Task tool` references in `ARCHITECTURE.md` are intentionally historical/example-specific or removed.

## Verification Criteria
- Search `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` for normative `Task tool` usage and confirm the canonical contract now uses `Subagent`.
- Read the updated sections and confirm they describe runtime-agnostic manager-pattern behavior with scoped context by default.
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md uses Subagent canonically, preserves D1-D8 without contradiction, and leaves no accidental normative Task tool references.
close_reason: Completed: rewrite ARCHITECTURE.md to define the canonical runtime-agnostic Subagent contract

title: Migrate Reviewing References To A Bead-Native Contract
description:
What:
Replace the file-based review artifact references with a bead-native reviewer contract.

Why:
The reviewing skill is only consistent if the prompts and templates used by reviewer agents also stop writing per-finding markdown files.

How:
Introduce a bead-native reference template for review findings, update review-agent-prompts.md so agents 1-4 emit bead-ready findings, and update the learnings-synthesizer contract so it can summarize around bead-created findings while keeping only the allowed session-level summary artifact. Repurpose finding-template.md into a short deprecation/pointer document that retires the old per-finding file contract and points readers to review-bead-template.md as the active replacement.

File Scope:
- khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/review-agent-prompts.md
- khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/finding-template.md
- khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/review-bead-template.md

Verification:
- rg -F -n -e "Write findings as files" -e "One finding file per distinct issue" -e "{id}-pending-{priority}-{slug}.md" -e "collect and paste all .khuym/findings/*.md content here" khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/*.md returns no matches (exit 1)
- rg -F -n "review-bead-template.md" khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/finding-template.md returns a pointer to the new active template
- bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh exits 0
notes:
Validated by spike br-2xp:
- Reviewer prompts and templates must instruct P2/P3 bead creation without parent-child linkage to the current epic.
- Use external_ref and review severity labels for traceability.
- P1 remains blocking and may stay in the current fix path.

title: Resolve Review P2: Remove duplicated normative rules across dream docs
description:
## Problem Statement

Dream behavior rules are split across `SKILL.md`, `codex-source-policy.md`, and `CREATION-LOG.md`, with multiple files restating normative requirements. That duplication increases drift risk because future edits can change one contract surface without updating the others.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 35-52, 101-108

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md`
**Line(s):** 7-45

```md
Both files define mode selection, source priority, and recurring-scan constraints in normative language.
```

**Why this is a problem:** when rule-bearing text is duplicated, review and maintenance become less reliable and the effective contract can drift.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Declare one canonical normative source and make the rest derivative
**Pros:** simplifies future edits and aligns with prior learnings about contract drift.
**Cons:** requires a small doc restructure.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Keep duplication but manually cross-check on every edit
**Pros:** avoids restructuring now.
**Cons:** fragile and review-dependent.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] One file is declared the canonical contract for dream behavior.
- [ ] Secondary docs reference or summarize that contract instead of restating rules independently.
- [ ] Cross-file wording for mode selection and scan constraints cannot drift silently.

> KNOWN PATTERN: Matches 20260325-docs-readme-portability.md
> Previous occurrence: `history/learnings/20260325-docs-readme-portability.md`
> Past resolution: identify an authoritative contract and require cross-checks for derivative docs.

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can one dependency helper parse current SKILL frontmatter and survive .codex reuse?
close_reason: YES: current Khuym skills share a parseable top-of-file frontmatter boundary, and onboarding can safely distribute a new .codex dependency helper via MANAGED_SUPPORT_FILES.

title: Run Dream Skill GREEN Verification And Refactor
description:
What:
Run the GREEN/REFACTOR pass for `khuym:dream`: execute the pressure scenarios against the written skill, capture any new rationalizations, and tighten the contract until it is defensible.

Why:
The dream skill is high-risk because it rewrites durable learnings and pauses for user choices. We need proof that the skill holds up under pressure, not just that it reads well.

How:
- Re-run the baseline pressure scenarios from `khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md` with the skill present.
- Update `khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` with GREEN and REFACTOR results, including any newly discovered loopholes.
- If a scenario still fails, revise `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md` and/or reference files to address the exact rationalization and re-run until the scenarios pass.
- Ensure the final contract explicitly prevents: silent bad merges, unbounded `.codex` scans on recurring runs, auto-edits to `critical-patterns.md`, silent guesses about first-run status, and vague ambiguity prompts with no candidate-specific options.
- Specifically verify that `last_dream_consolidated_at` is the documented provenance field used to distinguish bootstrap from recurring behavior.

File Scope:
- khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/consolidation-rubric.md
- khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md

Verification:
- `rg -n "GREEN|REFACTOR|Iteration|rationalization" khuym/khuym:dream/CREATION-LOG.md` finds post-RED results
- `rg -n "critical-patterns.md|7 days|20 sessions|last_dream_consolidated_at|merge|create new|skip|no durable signal|candidate learnings files" khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md khuym/khuym:dream/references/*.md` finds the final guardrails
- The documented pressure scenarios all have recorded outcomes in `CREATION-LOG.md`
notes:
Validated spike findings to preserve during GREEN/REFACTOR:
- Keep the provenance-based bootstrap/recurring contract intact; do not let recurring runs silently expand to full-scan behavior.
- Keep rewrite as the narrowest path; any ambiguity must pause for explicit user choice.
- Ensure the ambiguity prompt includes candidate files, reasons, and labeled options rather than a generic free-form question.
close_reason: Completed: Ran dream GREEN/REFACTOR verification and tightened guardrails from scenario replay

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Should Agent Mail dependencies model am or the MCP tool contract?
close_reason: NO: Agent Mail is exposed here as MCP tool operations, not a truthful am shell command or repo-local Node-readable probe contract, so Phase 1 must be replanned before execution.

title: Align swarming subagent docs across both skill trees
description:
## What
Update the swarming skill docs and worker templates so the operational spawning language matches the canonical `Subagent` contract.

## Why
Swarming is the main operational surface where the old `Task tool` terminology still appears, and this repo has both `.agents/skills/` and `skills/` copies that are already divergent.

## How
Align the wording in both skill trees unless execution proves one tree is generated from the other. Preserve actual swarming behavior while updating terminology, manager-pattern framing, scoped-context assumptions, and any worker-spawn wording that would contradict D1-D8.

## File Scope
- `.agents/skills/swarming/SKILL.md`
- `.agents/skills/swarming/references/worker-template.md`
- `skills/swarming/SKILL.md`
- `skills/swarming/references/worker-template.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Swarming docs in both skill trees use terminology that matches the canonical `Subagent` contract.
- Worker-template wording still preserves explicit identity, scope, setup, and executor guidance.
- The implementation applies an explicit sync strategy across both skill trees instead of leaving mixed terminology behind.

## Verification Criteria
- Search both swarming trees for conflicting `Task tool` language and confirm the remaining operational wording is intentionally aligned.
- Read the worker templates to confirm the execution guidance remains explicit and actionable.
acceptance_criteria: Swarming docs in both skill trees align with the canonical Subagent contract, preserve explicit worker identity/scope/setup guidance, and apply an intentional sync strategy.
close_reason: Completed: align swarming Subagent wording across both skill trees

title: Validate README wording against architecture and published metadata
description:
What:
Run a final consistency pass on README.md so its wording matches the repo's real workflow, package layout, plugin inventory, and install mechanics.

Why:
The main implementation risk is not code complexity but accidental doc drift or overclaiming. This bead reduces that risk before review.

How:
Cross-check README.md against docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/IMPLEMENTATION_ALIGNMENT_REVIEW.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and scripts/sync-skills.sh. Tighten any language that implies stronger implementation alignment than the repo currently documents.

File Scope:
- README.md

Verification Criteria:
- Workflow and gate descriptions match architecture
- Install, plugin, and sync commands match the manifests/scripts exactly
- README does not overstate areas marked divergent in IMPLEMENTATION_ALIGNMENT_REVIEW.md
- Final wording remains calm, technical, and aligned with D3 and D4
close_reason: Completed: finalized README wording against architecture, alignment review, manifests, and sync commands

title: Define Subagent Architecture Contract
description:
## Success Criteria
- The canonical architecture doc defines the concept as `Subagent`.
- The directly affected swarming and reviewing docs align with the canonical term.
- Validation confirms migration boundaries are explicit and executable.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical architecture doc defines Subagent, the directly affected swarming/reviewing docs align with it, and migration boundaries are explicit and executable.
close_reason: Feature complete: Subagent architecture contract clarified and direct operational docs aligned

title: Resolve Review P2: Define precedence for marker-absent recurring overrides
description:
## Problem Statement

The contract currently says missing provenance implies bootstrap, but also says conflicting provenance and user intent should trigger one clarification question. That leaves marker-absent + recurring request behavior underspecified.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/codex-source-policy.md`
**Line(s):** 17-18, 26-39

**Why this is a problem:** two plausible implementations remain, which weakens determinism for D2.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The contract includes a single precedence table for marker absence, bootstrap override, and recurring override.
- [ ] Clarification behavior is explicitly tied to only one defined conflict case.

title: Refactor Reviewing To Use Bead-Native Findings
description:
What:
Refactor the Khuym reviewing workflow so review findings are tracked as beads instead of per-finding markdown files.

Why:
Locked decisions D1-D7 require bead-native review outputs while preserving P1 as the blocking tier and allowing P2/P3 follow-up work to be scheduled for later.

How:
Implement the contract described in history/review-findings-as-beads/approach.md with these non-negotiable outcomes:
- P1 findings create blocking fix beads in the current review/epic-close path.
- P2 and P3 findings always create follow-up beads, but those beads must stay outside the current epic-close path.
- P2/P3 traceability uses external_ref plus review severity labels rather than parent-child linkage to the current epic.
- Full review detail lives in the bead body instead of a per-finding markdown artifact.
- The active per-finding markdown file contract is removed from reviewing and its references.

Verification:
- br show br-2lb and br show br-3by both report CLOSED
- rg -F -n -e "Write findings as files" -e "One finding file per distinct issue" -e "{id}-pending-{priority}-{slug}.md" khuym/khuym:reviewing/SKILL.md khuym/khuym:reviewing/references/*.md returns no matches (exit 1)
- bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh exits 0
- claude plugin validate . exits 0

title: Resolve Review P2: Constrain recurring override schema and bounds
description:
## Problem Statement

The dream contract allows recurring overrides by days and sessions, but it never defines valid ranges, types, or rejection behavior for malformed values. That makes the scan window contract under-specified and creates room for accidental unbounded reads or inconsistent operator handling.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/SKILL.md`
**Line(s):** 27-29, 50, 97

```md
- Optional recurring override: days and/or sessions
...
3. Recurring defaults: last `7 days` and up to `20 sessions`, unless user override is provided.
```

**Why this is a problem:** without explicit bounds and validation, overrides can silently weaken D2's bounded recurring-scan contract.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Define validated override schema and fallback behavior
**Pros:** makes recurring scope deterministic and protects against accidental full scans.
**Cons:** adds a little contract detail.
**Effort:** Small

### Option B — Leave overrides free-form and rely on operator judgment
**Pros:** shorter docs.
**Cons:** keeps safety and determinism implicit.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The contract defines accepted recurring override fields and expected value types.
- [ ] Invalid or extreme override values are rejected or clarified instead of being applied silently.
- [ ] Recurring overrides cannot bypass the no-unbounded-scan rule without explicit bootstrap/full-scan intent.

title: using-khuym Library Validation Checks
description:
What:
Add dependency validation to `khuym:using-khuym` so Khuym can read declared tool requirements from its own skills, detect missing tools such as `br`, `bv`, `cass`, `cm`, `gkg`, or `am`, and report which skills are degraded or unavailable.

Why:
Locked decisions D1-D5 require Khuym to warn without hard-stopping bootstrap, aggregate dependency data from per-skill `SKILL.md` declarations, and surface the same dependency state in scout/bootstrap/startup flows.

How:
Implement the approved plan in `history/using-khuym-lib-validation/phase-plan.md` across these phases:
- Phase 1: Core Dependency Report
- Phase 2: Bootstrap And Startup Warnings
- Phase 3: Full Coverage And Regression Lock-In

Verification:
- Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 child tasks are closed with their stated verification.
- `bash scripts/sync-skills.sh --dry-run` exits 0.
- `bash scripts/check-markdown-links.sh` exits 0.
- `claude plugin validate .` exits 0.

title: Add Khuym scout CLI and state mirror
description: Implement the shipped Khuym vNext ergonomics/runtime update in this repo: add .khuym/state.json support, install a read-only scout CLI via onboarding, update AGENTS.template and core skill docs to use the scout/state flow, add canonical architecture + golden-path docs, and restore the repo maintenance scripts for markdown-link checks and skill sync.
close_reason: Completed

title: Resolve Review P1: Add branch-complete pressure coverage for dream outcomes
description:
## Problem Statement

The implemented pressure scenarios do not cover all four branches of the promised consolidation rubric. `no match` and `no durable signal` are core behavior branches in the shipped contract, but they are not explicitly pressure-tested or logged in GREEN/REFACTOR.

## Evidence

**File:** `khuym/khuym:dream/references/pressure-scenarios.md`
**Line(s):** 5-111

```md
Scenario coverage today focuses on bootstrap, multi-match rewrite, ambiguity prompt quality, critical-pattern gating, and a combined-pressure case.
```

**Why this is a problem:** missing two rubric branches leaves creation-vs-no-write behavior under-tested in a skill that rewrites durable memory.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Add RED/GREEN scenarios for both missing branches
**Pros:** closes the highest-risk coverage gap using the repo's existing skill-TDD pattern.
**Cons:** requires updating both scenario catalog and creation log.
**Effort:** Medium

### Option B — Only document the branches without pressure scenarios
**Pros:** faster.
**Cons:** does not satisfy the current claim of branch-tested guardrails.
**Effort:** Small

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pressure scenarios include `no match` and `no durable signal`.
- [ ] `CREATION-LOG.md` records GREEN/REFACTOR outcomes for both branches.
- [ ] Verification language in the skill and log matches the actual scenario coverage.
notes: ScarletBeaver: released claim due file reservations held by RusticCompass; waiting coordinator routing.
close_reason: Completed: added no-match/no-durable-signal pressure scenarios and logged GREEN/REFACTOR outcomes
